   613→pub(crate) async fn render_spa_template(
   614→    gateway: &GatewayState,
   615→    template: SpaTemplate,
   616→) -> axum::response::Response {
   617→    let (build_ts, asset_prefix) = if is_dev_assets() {
   618→        let ts = std::time::SystemTime::now()
   619→            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   620→            .unwrap_or_default()
   621→            .as_millis();
   622→        ("dev".to_owned(), format!("/assets/v/{ts}/"))
   623→    } else {
   624→        static HASH: std::sync::LazyLock<String> = std::sync::LazyLock::new(asset_content_hash);
   625→        (HASH.to_string(), format!("/assets/v/{}/", *HASH))
   626→    };
   627→
   628→    let nonce = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
   629→
   630→    // Resolve Shiki URL from config override or default CDN.
   631→    let shiki_url = gateway
   632→        .inner
   633→        .read()
   634→        .await
   635→        .shiki_cdn_url
   636→        .clone()
   637→        .unwrap_or_else(|| DEFAULT_SHIKI_CDN_URL.to_owned());
   638→
   639→    let body = match template {
   640→        SpaTemplate::Index => {
   641→            let gon = build_gon_data(gateway).await;
   642→            let share_meta = build_share_meta(&gon.identity);
   643→            let gon_json = script_safe_json(&gon);
   644→            let template = IndexHtmlTemplate {
   645→                build_ts: &build_ts,
   646→                asset_prefix: &asset_prefix,
   647→                nonce: &nonce,
   648→                gon_json: &gon_json,
   649→                share_title: &share_meta.title,
   650→                share_description: &share_meta.description,
   651→                share_site_name: &share_meta.site_name,
   652→                share_image_url: SHARE_IMAGE_URL,
   653→                share_image_alt: &share_meta.image_alt,
   654→                routes: &SPA_ROUTES,
   655→                shiki_url: &shiki_url,
   656→            };
   657→            match template.render() {
   658→                Ok(html) => html,
   659→                Err(e) => {
   660→                    warn!(error = %e, "failed to render index template");
   661→                    String::new()
   662→                },
   663→            }
   664→        },
   665→        SpaTemplate::Login => {
   666→            let gon = build_gon_data(gateway).await;
   667→            let gon_json = script_safe_json(&gon);
   668→            let page_title = identity_name(&gon.identity).to_owned();
   669→            let template = LoginHtmlTemplate {
   670→                build_ts: &build_ts,
   671→                asset_prefix: &asset_prefix,
   672→                nonce: &nonce,
   673→                page_title: &page_title,
   674→                gon_json: &gon_json,
   675→            };
   676→            match template.render() {
   677→                Ok(html) => html,
   678→                Err(e) => {
   679→                    warn!(error = %e, "failed to render login template");
   680→                    String::new()
   681→                },
   682→            }
   683→        },
   684→        SpaTemplate::Onboarding => {
   685→            let gon = build_gon_data(gateway).await;
   686→            let gon_json = script_safe_json(&gon);
   687→            let page_title = format!("{} onboarding", identity_name(&gon.identity));
   688→            let template = OnboardingHtmlTemplate {
   689→                build_ts: &build_ts,
   690→                asset_prefix: &asset_prefix,
   691→                nonce: &nonce,
   692→                page_title: &page_title,
   693→                gon_json: &gon_json,
   694→            };
   695→            match template.render() {
   696→                Ok(html) => html,
   697→                Err(e) => {
   698→                    warn!(error = %e, "failed to render onboarding template");
   699→                    String::new()
   700→                },
   701→            }
   702→        },
   703→        SpaTemplate::SetupRequired => {
   704→            let template = SetupRequiredHtmlTemplate {
   705→                asset_prefix: &asset_prefix,
   706→            };
   707→            match template.render() {
   708→                Ok(html) => html,
   709→                Err(e) => {
   710→                    warn!(error = %e, "failed to render setup-required template");
   711→                    String::new()
   712→                },
   713→            }
   714→        },
   715→    };
   716→
   717→    // Extract CDN origin from shiki_url for CSP script-src allowlisting.
   718→    let shiki_csp_origin = url::Url::parse(&shiki_url)
   719→        .ok()
   720→        .and_then(|u| u.host_str().map(|host| format!("{}://{host}", u.scheme())));
   721→
   722→    let csp = format!(
   723→        "default-src 'self'; \
   724→         script-src 'self' 'nonce-{nonce}' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'{shiki_origin}; \
   725→         style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; \
   726→         img-src 'self' data: blob:; \
   727→         media-src 'self' blob:; \
   728→         font-src 'self'; \
   729→         connect-src 'self' ws: wss:; \
   730→         frame-ancestors 'none'; \
   731→         form-action 'self'; \
   732→         base-uri 'self'; \
   733→         object-src 'none'",
   734→        shiki_origin = shiki_csp_origin
   735→            .as_deref()
   736→            .map(|o| format!(" {o}"))
   737→            .unwrap_or_default(),
   738→    );
   739→
   740→    let mut response = Html(body).into_response();
   741→    let headers = response.headers_mut();
   742→    if let Ok(val) = "no-cache, no-store".parse() {
   743→        headers.insert(axum::http::header::CACHE_CONTROL, val);
   744→    }
   745→    if let Ok(val) = csp.parse() {
   746→        headers.insert(axum::http::header::CONTENT_SECURITY_POLICY, val);
   747→    }
   748→    response
   749→}

1→//! Live CRM service backed by the SQLite store.
     2→//!
     3→//! [`LiveCrmService`] implements [`moltis_service_traits::CrmService`] by
     4→//! delegating to [`moltis_crm::SqliteCrmStore`].  Each RPC method deserialises
     5→//! its `serde_json::Value` params, calls the appropriate store operation, and
     6→//! serialises the result back.
     7→//!
     8→//! PII fields (email, phone) are exposed in responses — callers reach this
     9→//! service only through the auth-gated WebSocket layer.
    10→
    11→use std::sync::Arc;
    12→
    13→use {
    14→    async_trait::async_trait,
    15→    moltis_crm::{
    16→        Contact, ContactChannel, Interaction, Matter, SqliteCrmStore,
    17→        store::CrmStore,
    18→        types::{ContactStage, InteractionKind, MatterPhase, MatterStatus, PracticeArea},
    19→    },
    20→    moltis_service_traits::{CrmService, ServiceError, ServiceResult},
    21→    secrecy::{ExposeSecret, Secret},
    22→    serde_json::Value,
    23→};
    24→
    25→/// Live CRM service backed by a SQLite store.
    26→pub struct LiveCrmService {
    27→    store: Arc<SqliteCrmStore>,
    28→}
    29→
    30→impl LiveCrmService {
    31→    #[must_use]
    32→    pub fn new(store: Arc<SqliteCrmStore>) -> Self {
    33→        Self { store }
    34→    }
    35→}
    36→
    37→// ── Serialisation helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    38→
    39→fn contact_to_json(c: Contact) -> Value {
    40→    serde_json::json!({
    41→        "id":         c.id,
    42→        "name":       c.name,
    43→        "source":     c.source,
    44→        "externalId": c.external_id,
    45→        "email":      c.email.as_ref().map(|s| s.expose_secret()),
    46→        "phone":      c.phone.as_ref().map(|s| s.expose_secret()),
    47→        "stage":      c.stage.as_str(),
    48→        "metadata":   c.metadata,
    49→        "createdAt":  c.created_at,
    50→        "updatedAt":  c.updated_at,
    51→    })
    52→}
    53→
    54→fn channel_to_json(ch: ContactChannel) -> Value {
    55→    serde_json::json!({
    56→        "id":          ch.id,
    57→        "contactId":   ch.contact_id,
    58→        "channelType": ch.channel_type,
    59→        "channelId":   ch.channel_id,
    60→        "displayName": ch.display_name,
    61→        "verified":    ch.verified,
    62→        "createdAt":   ch.created_at,
    63→        "updatedAt":   ch.updated_at,
    64→    })
    65→}
    66→
    67→fn matter_to_json(m: Matter) -> Value {
    68→    serde_json::json!({
    69→        "id":           m.id,
    70→        "contactId":    m.contact_id,
    71→        "title":        m.title,
    72→        "description":  m.description,
    73→        "status":       m.status.as_str(),
    74→        "phase":        m.phase.as_str(),
    75→        "practiceArea": m.practice_area.as_str(),
    76→        "createdAt":    m.created_at,
    77→        "updatedAt":    m.updated_at,
    78→    })
    79→}
    80→
    81→fn interaction_to_json(i: Interaction) -> Value {
    82→    serde_json::json!({
    83→        "id":        i.id,
    84→        "contactId": i.contact_id,
    85→        "matterId":  i.matter_id,
    86→        "kind":      i.kind.as_str(),
    87→        "summary":   i.summary,

Actually, looking at how ES modules work, this should be fine - even though `page-crm.js` gets imported twice, it's only evaluated once and the cached version is reused on subsequent imports. The side effect that registers the CRM prefix runs during that first evaluation, so the behavior is correct regardless of import order. The explicit import in `app.js` is redundant now but keeping it is clearer for maintainability. For the `navCountCrm` badge, I need to figure out where to render it in the DOM so that `updateNavCount("crm", n)` can find and update it—likely adding a span element inside the nav button itself. I could add a `badgeId` property to the section definition and update the nav rendering to conditionally display the badge span, but that's expanding scope. Let me keep this minimal and just leave the nav link as-is for now—the badge functionality can be a separate concern.
